source: Atomic Design (Brad Frost), adapted for modern component libraries

dualSystem.keyInsight: These are orthogonal. A component has both a domain (location) AND an atomic level (complexity).

ATOM.description: No composition, standalone UI elements. The most basic building blocks.

MOLECULE.description: Simple groups of atoms working together. Fixed sub-parts with defined structure.

ORGANISM.description: Complex sections with business logic. Self-contained interface components.

TEMPLATE.description: Page structure without content. Defines the wireframe and layout grid.

PAGE.description: Template filled with actual data. Complete user-facing screens.

compositionRule: Higher levels use lower levels. Composition flows downward only. Never create upward dependencies.

Shared.contains: App Shell, Data displays, Feedback, Navigation, Layout

decisionTree.question: Is it a UI primitive (button, input, badge)?
Is it used by multiple products?
Is it only for one specific product?

componentStructure.example:
ComponentName/
├── index.ts
├── ComponentName.tsx
├── ComponentName.stories
├── ComponentName.test
├── ComponentName.css
└── README.md

compoundComponents.description: For molecules with sub-components, export all parts from index.ts
compoundComponents.example:
Dialog/
├── index.ts (exports Dialog, DialogHeader, DialogContent, DialogFooter)
├── Dialog.tsx
├── DialogHeader.tsx
├── DialogContent.tsx
└── DialogFooter.tsx

stateManagement.rule: State scope should match component level. Don't use global state for atom behavior.

do: Composition flows DOWN - Higher levels use lower levels only
Respect domain boundaries - Product-specific stays in Products/
Label every component - Include atomic type in documentation
Use atomic classification consistently - Label must match actual complexity
dont: Create upward dependencies - Never import from higher levels
Mix domains - Don't put product-specific logic in Core
Skip documentation - Stories need both domain navigation and atomic labels
Create hybrid components - Don't mix page-level concerns into organisms

antiPatterns.problem: Organism that tries to do everything

antiPatterns.fix: Keep public APIs clean and stable

antiPatterns.problem: Product-specific logic in Shared or Core
antiPatterns.fix: Keep boundaries clear, move to Products/

checklist: Classify by domain first - Core, Shared, or Products?
Determine atomic level - ATOM through PAGE?
Create proper file structure - Follow component file patterns
Write the story - Include domain navigation and atomic label
Document the API - Props, variants, usage examples
Add tests - Unit tests for atoms/molecules, integration for organisms
Export cleanly - Use barrel exports for public API

quickReference.sharedOrProducts: If >1 product uses it -> Shared/. Otherwise -> Products/
quickReference.stateManagementForMolecule: Local state only. No global state for simple components.
